Easy to misuse or abuse

title: Building a Neon Glow PPTX Template with Python
text:
We needed a PPTX template inspired by modern SaaS dashboard aesthetics — dark backgrounds, neon glow effects, and a futuristic feel. The reference was an n8n 2026 roadmap infographic with fluorescent color accents on a deep navy background.
The question: Can PowerPoint natively render neon glow effects without resorting to raster images?

title: Building a Neon Glow PPTX Template with Python
text:
We needed a PPTX template inspired by modern SaaS dashboard aesthetics — dark backgrounds, neon glow effects, and a futuristic feel. The reference was an n8n 2026 roadmap infographic with fluorescent color accents on a deep navy background.
The question: Can PowerPoint natively render neon glow effects without resorting to raster images?

title: Building a Neon Glow PPTX Template with Python
Building a Neon Glow PPTX Template with Python
text:
Design Principle: Frames, Not Lines
Our initial approach used solid-fill rectangles (add_rect) with a:glow. This worked in PowerPoint but had a problem: LibreOffice doesn’t render a:glow on solid-fill shapes accurately. The glow radius changes were nearly invisible in LibreOffice-exported PNGs.
The fix: replace all line/bar decorations with thin hollow rounded-rect frames (add_neon_frame). A neon frame is:

No fill (a:noFill)
Colored border (a:ln with a:solidFill)
Glow on the border (a:glow in a:effectLst)

LibreOffice renders glow on bordered shapes much more faithfully than on filled shapes.

title: Building a Neon Glow PPTX Template with Python
text:
Layout Decoration Rules
All 7 Pandoc PPTX layouts use neon frames exclusively:

Single-content layouts (Title Slide, Title and Content, Section Header): Thin elongated frames mimicking decorative lines
Multi-column layouts (Two Content, Comparison, Content with Caption): Normal-sized panel frames

This keeps the visual language consistent — every glowing element is structurally a frame.

title: Building a Neon Glow PPTX Template with Python
text:
PNG Export Pipeline
For the website showcase, we convert PPTX slides to PNG images:

build_template_roadmap.py → template-roadmap.pptx
quarto render → demo-roadmap.pptx
postprocess_demo.py → fixes vertical centering
LibreOffice headless → PDF
pdftoppm → individual PNG per slide (200 DPI)

This pipeline runs locally. The PNGs are committed to the website repo and displayed in a JavaScript-powered slide gallery with keyboard navigation.

title: Building a Neon Glow PPTX Template with Python
text:
Key Takeaway
Native OOXML effects (a:glow, a:outerShdw) are powerful but renderer-dependent. If your output needs to look consistent across PowerPoint, LibreOffice, and Google Slides, use bordered hollow shapes rather than solid-fill shapes as glow carriers. The border gives renderers a clear edge to apply the glow effect to.

title: AI Operation Methodology
text:
AI Operation is not about running AI models. It’s about designing how humans and AI collaborate in structured, repeatable, and improvable ways.

AI Operation = Designing how humans structure tasks, workflows, and roles to make AI a controllable, learnable, and evolvable partner.

title: AI Operation Methodology
text:
AI Operation is not about running AI models. It’s about designing how humans and AI collaborate in structured, repeatable, and improvable ways.

AI Operation = Designing how humans structure tasks, workflows, and roles to make AI a controllable, learnable, and evolvable partner.

title: AI Operation Methodology
text:
Core Question
The central question of AI Operation is:

Given a task, how should we divide work between humans and AI, structure the workflow, define quality criteria, and enable continuous improvement?

title: AI Operation Methodology
text:
Three Pillars

1. Task Design

Decomposition — Break complex work into AI-executable steps
Role Assignment — Define what humans do vs. what AI does
Quality Boundaries — Set acceptance criteria for AI outputs
Error Handling — Design feedback loops for when AI gets it wrong



2. Process Architecture

Pipeline Design — Chain multiple AI steps into a coherent workflow
Human Checkpoints — Where humans review, approve, or redirect
State Management — Track what’s done, what’s pending, what failed
Iteration Protocol — How to improve outputs across cycles



3. Capability Evolution

Skill Modules — Encapsulate proven AI workflows as reusable modules
Knowledge Codification — Turn tribal knowledge into machine-readable reference docs
Maturity Assessment — Measure how effectively the organization uses AI
Continuous Learning — Update skills and processes as AI capabilities evolve

AI 不適合一開始就進入生產系統，原因包括：

容易誤用、濫用
無法觀察決策過程
缺乏回饋與修正機制
組織成員尚未具備使用素養


AI Operation Lab 的定位：

AI 的駕訓班，而不是高速公路。

在這裡你可以：

安全嘗試
重複實驗
理解 AI 行為
建立正確使用習慣

text:
我們需要一個受現代 SaaS 儀表板美學啟發的 PPTX 模板 — 深色背景、霓虹發光效果、未來感。參考對象是一張 n8n 2026 Roadmap 資訊圖，在深海軍藍背景上有螢光色彩強調。

title: 用 Python 建構霓虹發光 PPTX 模板
用 Python 建構霓虹發光 PPTX 模板
text:
設計原則：用框，不用線
最初我們用實色填充矩形（add_rect）+ a:glow。在 PowerPoint 裡效果正確，但有個問題：LibreOffice 對實色填充形狀的 a:glow 渲染不精確，匯出的 PNG 幾乎看不出光暈大小差異。
解決方案：把所有直線/條狀裝飾替換為細長的空心圓角框（add_neon_frame）。霓虹框的結構是：

無填充（a:noFill）
有色邊框（a:ln + a:solidFill）
邊框上的光暈（a:effectLst 裡的 a:glow）

LibreOffice 對有邊框形狀的光暈渲染遠比實色填充形狀精確。

title: AI Operation 方法論
text:
AI Operation 不是指運行 AI 模型。它是關於設計人類與 AI 如何以結構化、可重複、可改善的方式協作。

AI Operation = 人類如何設計任務、流程與角色，讓 AI 成為可控、可學、可成長的夥伴。

title: AI Operation 方法論
text:
AI Operation 不是指運行 AI 模型。它是關於設計人類與 AI 如何以結構化、可重複、可改善的方式協作。

AI Operation = 人類如何設計任務、流程與角色，讓 AI 成為可控、可學、可成長的夥伴。

title: AI Operation 方法論
text:
核心問題
AI Operation 的核心問題是：

面對一個任務，我們應該如何分配人與 AI 的工作、設計工作流程、定義品質標準、並實現持續改善？

title: AI Operation 方法論
text:
三大支柱

1. 任務設計（Task Design）

任務拆解 — 將複雜工作拆成 AI 可執行的步驟
角色分配 — 定義人與 AI 各自負責什麼
品質邊界 — 設定 AI 產出的驗收標準
錯誤處理 — 設計 AI 出錯時的回饋機制



2. 流程架構（Process Architecture）

Pipeline 設計 — 將多個 AI 步驟串聯成連貫的工作流程
人工檢核點 — 人類在哪裡審查、批准或重新導向
狀態管理 — 追蹤已完成、待處理、已失敗的項目
迭代協定 — 如何在每次循環中改善產出



3. 能力進化（Capability Evolution）

技能模組 — 將已驗證的 AI 工作流程封裝為可複用的模組
知識編碼化 — 將隱性知識轉化為機器可讀的參考文件
成熟度評估 — 衡量組織使用 AI 的有效程度
持續學習 — 隨 AI 能力演進更新技能和流程

title: AI Operation 方法論
text:

症狀

「AI 幫不了我們」

沒有品質邊界


「無法擴展 AI 使用」
沒有流程架構


「總是從頭開始」
沒有能力進化

title: 核心原則
text:
這些原則指導 AI Cooperation 框架中的每個決策。


AI agent 在明確的邊界內表現最好。定義輸入、輸出和約束不會限制創造力 — 它能產生可靠、可重複的結果。




Skills（技能） 定義 agent 應該做什麼
Reference docs（參考文件） 定義如何做
Prompts（提示） 定義 agent 是誰

這種分離允許每個組件獨立進化。



AI 應該增強人的能力，而不是取代人的判斷。每個 pipeline 都應該有明確的人工檢核點，用於審查、批准或重新導向。



複雜的 AI 工作流程應該由小型、可複用、可獨立測試的模組組成。每個模組遵循標準化結構和清晰的合約。



理解一個模組的最好方式是運行它的範例。每個技能都必須包含可運行的工作範例。



AI 能力變化迅速。框架設計為可進化的：

模組使用語意化版本控制（semver）
知識庫可以獨立更新
Pipeline 可以重新配置而無需重寫技能




所有技能模組、參考文件和 pipeline 定義應該：

版本控制
人類可讀
機器可解析
盡可能開源

title: 什麼是 AI Operation？
text:
大多數人談 AI，談的是模型、工具、技術。
但真正的問題是：你會用 AI 工作嗎？
不是「會不會下 prompt」，而是：

你能把一個複雜任務拆解成 AI 可執行的步驟嗎？
你知道哪些部分該交給 AI，哪些該自己做嗎？
你有辦法評估 AI 的產出品質嗎？
你能設計一個讓 AI 持續改善的流程嗎？

如果你覺得這些問題很難回答，那你需要的不是更好的 AI 工具——你需要的是 AI Operation。

title: 什麼是 AI Operation？
text:
AI Operation 是什麼？

AI Operation 是一套設計「人 × AI × 流程」如何協作的系統方法。

它包含三個核心面向：

1. Task Design（任務設計）
把複雜工作拆解成 AI 可處理的步驟。定義每個步驟的輸入、輸出、品質標準。


2. Process Architecture（流程架構）
設計多步驟的 pipeline，包含 AI 執行步驟和人類審核點。管理狀態、處理錯誤、支持迭代。


3. Capability Evolution（能力進化）
把成功的 AI 工作模式封裝成可複用的模組。持續更新知識庫，量化組織 AI 成熟度。

title: 什麼是 AI Operation？
text:
舉個例子
假設你要做一份簡報。
沒有 AI Operation： → 打開 ChatGPT，說「幫我做一份關於 XX 的簡報」 → 得到一堆文字 → 手動貼到 PowerPoint → 花好幾個小時調格式
有 AI Operation： → Planner Agent 分析需求，產出結構化規格（deck_spec.json） → Worker Agent 根據規格和 layout 規則，產出正確格式的 QMD → Pipeline 自動建構模板、渲染、後處理 → 輸出專業風格的 PPTX
差別不在 AI 的能力，而在操作設計。

title: AI Operation 架構
text:
核心概念

技能模組
每個 AI 能力都封裝為一個 Skill 模組 — 一個獨立的單元，包含：

合約（module.yaml）— 輸入、輸出、依賴、pipeline 步驟
技能 — AI agent 行為規格
參考文件 — 領域知識庫
工具 — 可執行腳本和程式
範例 — 可運行的示範

查看技能模組 →


Pipeline 架構
複雜任務被分解為多步驟 pipeline：
使用者請求
    ↓
[規劃 Skill] → 結構化計畫 (JSON)
    ↓
[工作 Skill] → 內容產出 (QMD, MD, Code...)
    ↓
[工具 Pipeline] → 最終成品 (PPTX, PDF, Deploy...)
    ↓
[人工審查] → 批准 / 迭代
每個步驟可以是 LLM 呼叫（skill）或工具執行（script）。


知識架構
prompts/         → 誰：agent 的身份（簡短、聚焦）
skills/          → 做什麼：agent 的行為（行為規格）
reference/       → 怎麼做：領域知識（參考文件）
tools/           → 執行：腳本、CLI
核心原則：Skills 指向 reference docs，不內嵌詳細知識。

為什麼大多數 AI 導入都失敗？


工具很多，但不知道怎麼用
企業買了 AI 工具卻用不上。缺乏將工具轉化為工作流程的方法。


員工會問 AI，但無法產出價值
個別使用 AI 的能力不等於組織層級的生產力。缺乏系統化的操作方法。


AI 專案難以評估 ROI
導入成效不明確，無法量化 AI 帶來的實際改善。


技術變化太快，導入風險高
每季都有新模型、新框架。組織不知道從哪裡開始，害怕押錯寶。

title: AI Cooperation
text:
什麼是 AI Operation？

AI Operation 是一套設計「人 × AI × 流程」如何協作的系統方法。
它不是指 AI 模型的運行，而是：人類如何設計任務、流程與角色，讓 AI 成為可控、可學、可成長的夥伴。

title: Core Principles
text:
These principles guide every decision in the AI Cooperation framework.


AI agents perform best within clear boundaries. Defining inputs, outputs, and constraints doesn’t limit creativity — it enables reliable, repeatable results.




Skills define WHAT the agent should do
Reference docs define HOW to do it
Prompts define WHO the agent is

This separation allows independent evolution of each component.



AI should augment human capability, not replace human judgment. Every pipeline should have explicit human checkpoints for review, approval, or redirection.



Complex AI workflows should be composed of small, reusable, independently testable modules. Each module follows a standardized structure with clear contracts.



The best way to understand a module is to run its examples. Every skill must include working, runnable examples.



AI capabilities change rapidly. The framework is designed for evolution:

Modules are versioned (semver)
Knowledge bases can be updated independently
Pipelines can be reconfigured without rewriting skills




All skill modules, reference documents, and pipeline definitions should be:

Version-controlled
Human-readable
Machine-parseable
Open source where possible

title: What is AI Operation?
text:
When most people talk about AI, they talk about models, tools, and technology.
But the real question is: Can you work with AI?
Not “can you write prompts,” but:

Can you decompose a complex task into AI-executable steps?
Do you know which parts to give AI and which to do yourself?
Can you evaluate the quality of AI outputs?
Can you design a process for AI to continuously improve?

If you find these questions hard to answer, what you need isn’t a better AI tool — what you need is AI Operation.

title: What is AI Operation?
text:
What is AI Operation?

AI Operation is a systematic approach to designing how humans, AI, and processes collaborate.

It encompasses three core dimensions:

1. Task Design
Decompose complex work into AI-processable steps. Define inputs, outputs, and quality standards for each step.


2. Process Architecture
Design multi-step pipelines with AI execution steps and human review points. Manage state, handle errors, support iteration.


3. Capability Evolution
Encapsulate successful AI work patterns into reusable modules. Continuously update knowledge bases and quantify organizational AI maturity.

title: What is AI Operation?
text:
An Example
Suppose you need to create a presentation.
Without AI Operation: → Open ChatGPT, say “make me a presentation about XX” → Get a bunch of text → Manually paste into PowerPoint → Spend hours formatting
With AI Operation: → Planner Agent analyzes requirements, produces structured spec (deck_spec.json) → Worker Agent generates correctly formatted QMD based on spec and layout rules → Pipeline automatically builds template, renders, and postprocesses → Outputs a professionally styled PPTX
The difference isn’t AI’s capability — it’s operation design.

title: AI Operation Framework
text:
Key Concepts

Skill Modules
Every AI capability is packaged as a Skill module — a self-contained unit with:

Contract (module.yaml) — Inputs, outputs, dependencies, pipeline steps
Skills — AI agent behavior specifications
Reference Docs — Domain knowledge base
Tools — Executable scripts and programs
Examples — Working demonstrations

View Skill Modules →


Pipeline Architecture
Complex tasks are broken into multi-step pipelines:
User Request
    ↓
[Planner Skill] → Structured Plan (JSON)
    ↓
[Worker Skill]  → Content Output (QMD, MD, Code...)
    ↓
[Tool Pipeline] → Final Artifact (PPTX, PDF, Deploy...)
    ↓
[Human Review]  → Approve / Iterate
Each step can be an LLM call (skill) or a tool execution (script).


Knowledge Architecture
prompts/         → WHO the agent is (short, focused)
skills/          → WHAT the agent does (behavior spec)
reference/       → HOW to do it (domain knowledge)
tools/           → EXECUTE (scripts, CLIs)
Key principle: Skills point to reference docs. They don’t inline detailed knowledge.

title: Skill Modules
text:
What is Skill?
We define a standard structure for AI skill modules:
skill-{name}/
├── module.yaml       # Contract: inputs, outputs, pipeline
├── skills/           # AI agent behavior specs
├── reference/        # Domain knowledge base
├── tools/            # Executable scripts
├── examples/         # Working demonstrations
└── README.md
Any AI framework that supports tool-use (LangChain, CrewAI, Claude, OpenAI Assistants) can integrate Skill modules.

title: AI Operation Lab
text:
Three-Layer Capability Building

L1 — Learning Environment

Dare to use, know how to use, don’t fear mistakes

Provide safe AI interaction spaces that simulate real work scenarios. All operations are traceable and replayable, letting learners build AI operation confidence in a zero-risk environment.
Solves: “I don’t know how to start”


L2 — Operation Method

Design AI work methods








Method
Description




Task Decomposition
Break complex work into AI-executable steps


Human-AI Division
Clearly define what humans and AI are each responsible for


Agent Collaboration
Design multi-agent cooperative logic


Decision Boundaries
Define the scope of AI autonomous decisions


Error Correction
Establish quality control mechanisms



Solves: “AI can’t really help me”


L3 — Evolution Path

Develop continuous evolution capability


AI usage maturity assessment
Organizational process restructuring
Long-term AI adoption roadmap
Talent and role redesign

Solves: “How to not be replaced by AI”
